purpose: CLI entry point and dispatcher for the BSE stage: solves the TDA Bethe-Salpeter Hamiltonian H = D + V − W in Ry, where D[b,c,v,k] = (ε_c[k,c] − ε_v[k,v])·X, V is the q=0 bare exchange applied in the ISDF centroid basis as (1/nk)·M†·V_q0·M with spin-traced pair amplitudes M[k,c,v,μ] = Σ_s ψ*_c ψ_v(r_μ), and W is the screened direct term applied as an FFT convolution (1/nk)Σ_{k'} W_{μν}(k−k') between per-vertex spinor-resolved pair densities. Routes to FEAST (default), KPM Chebyshev DOS, or the validated Lanczos/Davidson preview (_preview_lanczos), loading all tensors from the gw_jax ISDF restart (isdf_tensors_*.h5), applying BGW eqp1.dat QP corrections and rank-1 q=0 head injection, and writing BGW-compliant eigenvectors.h5 (valence flip + Ry→eV at the write boundary only). Also hosts a file-local jit'd matvec trio (apply_bse_hamiltonian/apply_V/apply_W) that is dead and non-executable, superseded by bse_serial/bse_ring_comm/bse_simple.
category_guess: pipeline stage — BSE eigensolver driver / CLI dispatcher (plus dead legacy matvec variant inside the same file)

purpose: Three files of the LORRAX ISDF-BSE matvec family (Henneke-2020 algorithm): bse_serial.py is the single-device reference TDA Hamiltonian H·X = (ε_c−ε_v)·X + V-term − W-term where the V (exchange) term is a centroid-basis contraction S[b,ν,k]=Σ_cv M[k,c,v,ν]X[b,c,v,k], U=V_q0·S, HX_V=conj(M)·U/nk, and the W (screened-direct) term is an FFT convolution U[k]=(1/√nk)Σ_k' W_{k−k'}[μ,ν]T[k'] on pair slabs T[b,μ,ν,t,s,k]=Σ_cv ψ_c[k,c,t,μ]conj(ψ_v[k,v,s,ν])X. bse_simple.py is the production distributed version of the identical math — plain jit + einsums + with_sharding_constraint on the (x,y) mesh (default --matvec-kind=simple), with XLA generating all collectives. bse_w_exact.py is a standalone CLI computing exact W_c(ω) columns in the ISDF centroid basis via shifted GMRES solves (z−H)x=f with the TDA or full non-TDA Casida matrix, one column per unit density source δ_{ν,ν0}, written to Wc_exact.h5 as the reference for the pseudopole-W program.
category_guess: pipeline stage (bse_serial = reference kernel, bse_simple = production matvec) + diagnostics (bse_w_exact = exact-W debug CLI, currently broken at HEAD)

features: Exact W_c(ω) columns via Casida shifted solves — src/bse/bse_w_exact.py:main (50-174)

purpose: Distributed BSE-Hamiltonian matvec kernels on a 2D (x,y) device mesh: conduction bands + ISDF centroid axis mu sharded on x, valence bands + centroid axis nu on y, with hand-rolled lax.ppermute rings / all_gather inside shard_map. Implements the TDA action HX = D + V - W with D[b,c,v,k]=(eps_c[k,c]-eps_v[k,v])X, the q=0 exchange V via S[b,nu,k]=Sum_{cvs} conj(psi_c[k,c,s,nu]) psi_v[k,v,s,nu] X[b,c,v,k]/sqrt(Nk), U=V_q0 S, VX=Sum_mu conj(M[k,c,v,mu])U/sqrt(Nk), and the direct W via T[b,mu,nu,t,s,k]=Sum_{cv} psi_c[k,c,t,mu] conj(psi_v[k,v,s,nu]) X, an ortho-normed 3D FFT convolution U(k)=(1/sqrt(Nk))Sum_{k'}W(k-k')T(k'), and back-projection through conj(psi_c), psi_v; also a full (non-TDA) two-block matvec S=[[A,B],[-B^H,-A^H]] and density-space probe operators for the W-exact/pseudopole tooling. It is the solver-agnostic core consumed by Lanczos/Haydock/Davidson/FEAST/KPM, and make_bse_shardings is the sharding vocabulary for the whole bse/ package.
category_guess: pipeline stage (distributed BSE matvec kernels) + variant axis (ring/gather/simple comm strategies, TDA vs full-BSE)

purpose: Lanczos-family eigensolvers for the TDA BSE. solvers/lanczos.py is physics-blind infra: four Hermitian lowest-eigenpair solvers (Python-loop simple, fori_loop jit with partial reorth, block-jit, and a lax.while_loop convergence-driven block variant) given only a matvec. bse/bse_lanczos.py wraps them with the BSE physics: it wires the TDA Hamiltonian H = D + V - W, with D[b,c,v,k] = (eps_c[k,c]-eps_v[k,v])X, V via ISDF pair amplitudes M[k,c,v,mu] = sum_s conj(psi_c[k,c,s,mu])psi_v[k,v,s,mu] contracted through V_q0[mu,nu] with 1/sqrt(Nk) on each side, and W via the T[b,mu,nu,t,s,k] tensor convolved with W_R = ifft_q(W_q) over the k-grid (ortho FFTs), then dispatches single-device (solve_bse) or 2D-mesh sharded (solve_bse_sharded, which also hosts the Davidson dispatch). Eigenvalues are exciton energies in Ry (BGW eV conversion and valence-axis flip live only at the bse_io writer). TDA-only and Hermitian-assumed; the CLI hard-gates --lanczos behind --tda.
category_guess: pipeline stage (BSE eigensolve driver) layered on generic solver infra (solvers.lanczos); audited at HEAD adc2197 = e18d0e5 for both files (git diff empty)

purpose: BSE data-plane I/O: loaders that turn the canonical gw_jax restart bundle (isdf_tensors_*.h5: flat-q V_qmunu/W0_qmunu (nq,mu,mu), psi_full_y (nk,nb,nspinor,n_rmu), enk_full, G0_mu_nu=zeta(q=0,mu,G=0), vhead/whead) into the psi/eps/V/W tensors the BSE solvers consume — one sharded (x,y)-mesh slab loader and one whole-array single-device ring loader. Physics: band slicing val=arange(n_occ-n_val,n_occ) (v=0 deepest, LORRAX-internal), q=0 Coulomb-head reinstatement as rank-1 V_q0[mu,nu] += v_scalar*conj(G0[mu])*G0[nu] (BGW mini-BZ 1/q^2 average, vhead=BGW wcoul0 in Ry), BGW eqp1.dat QP substitution enk_qp[k,b]=e_qp_ibz[irr_idx_k[k],b]/13.6056980659, and flat-q->3D-k W reshape W_q[mu,nu,i,j,l]=W_flat[i*nky*nkz+j*nkz+l,mu,nu]. Also two BGW-format eigenvectors.h5 writers: the streaming one converts Ry->eV and flips the valence axis (file[0,i,k,c,v]=vec[k,c,nv-1-v], BGW BSE/input_fi.f90:407 convention); the legacy one does neither.
category_guess: pipeline stage (BSE input assembly + BGW-compat eigenvector output); the sharded/ring loader pair is also a variant axis

purpose: FEAST interior-eigenvalue solver for the sharded ISDF-BSE Hamiltonian H = D + V − W (D_term[b,c,v,k] = (eps_c[k,c] − eps_v[k,v])·X[b,c,v,k]; exchange V and screened-direct W applied via the ring/all-gather ISDF matvec from bse_ring_comm). It estimates spectral bounds with a short Lanczos run (α = Re⟨q,Hq⟩ recurrence, adaptive E_max stop), builds eV energy windows (default, user, or KPM-DOS-derived), approximates the spectral projector P = (1/2πi)∮(zI−H)⁻¹dz with ellipse-trapezoid or Zolotarev rational quadrature, filters random/Ritz vectors through diagonal-preconditioned FGMRES shifted solves y = (z/s − H)⁻¹x (nodes/weights eV, H in Ry, s = 13.6056980659), and extracts eigenpairs by overlap-whitened Rayleigh-Ritz with full-space residuals Re[c†(G − 2λH + λ²S)c]/Re[c†Sc]. Despite the 'setup utilities' docstring it is the DEFAULT solver path of the BSE driver: bse_jax.main dispatches to bse_feast.main whenever --lanczos is not given. Handles both TDA (Hermitian, conjugate-symmetry contour, real-vector assumption) and full non-TDA ([[A,B],[−B†,−A†]], conjugate-doubled nodes, complex vectors); non-TDA is the CLI default.
category_guess: pipeline stage (default BSE eigensolver stage reached via bse_jax dispatch) with a diagnostics/report layer; also the shared kernel library for the feast/pseudopole sweep diagnostics

purpose: Four standalone `python -m bse.*` experiment drivers from the FEAST BSE-eigensolver development phase. They solve/validate the TDA-BSE eigenproblem H X = E X with H = D + V − W in the ISDF basis (D_{cvk} = eps_c[k,c] − eps_v[k,v]; pair amplitude M[k,c,v,μ] = Σ_s conj(ψ_c[k,c,s,μ])ψ_v[k,v,s,μ]; V/W applied via V_q0[μν] and FFT-convolved W_q(μν)), using the FEAST contour filter P ≈ Σ_j 2·Re[w_j (z_j I − H)^{-1} X] with ellipse quadrature z_j = c + r_x cosθ_j + i γ r_x sinθ_j, w_j = (1/2n)(r_y cosθ_j + i r_x sinθ_j) or Zolotarev step-difference poles ([w_L, −w_R] for h(λ)=step(λ−a)−step(λ−b)), followed by Rayleigh-Ritz extraction, sweeping n_quad/γ/rho_scale/n_ritz/feast_iter/gmres_tol against exact dense-diag eigenvalues. Windows/bounds are in eV while the matvec runs in Ry; the runner rescales z and w by ry_to_ev, keeping residues invariant (LORRAX-internal band order, no BGW valence flip). Three of the four are broken at HEAD (IndentationError; stale _get_feast_runner/run_feast_ritz APIs) and all four have zero callers — their functionality was absorbed into bse_feast.main().
category_guess: diagnostics — FEAST parameter-exploration sweep drivers, stale/abandoned (deleted as dead in a0da0a5, restored already-broken in fe5e3e8 same day 2026-04-29); superseded by bse_feast.main()'s --quadrature/n_quad-schedule/rho_scale

purpose: Stochastic Kernel Polynomial Method (KPM) density-of-states for the BSE Hamiltonian, plus the physics-free Chebyshev/DOS library underneath it. bse_kpm rescales the BSE operator to H_tilde=(H-c)/h with spectrum in [-1,1], runs the three-term Chebyshev recurrence T_p = 2*H_tilde*T_{p-1} - T_{p-2} on masked Rademacher vectors over the padded (b=1,c,v,k) transition space, estimates mu_p = (1/(R*D)) sum_r Re<x_r|T_p(H_tilde)|x_r>, Jackson-damps with sigma_p = [(M-p+1)cos(pi*p/(M+1)) + sin(pi*p/(M+1))/tan(pi/(M+1))]/(M+1), and reconstructs rho(E) = [mu_0 + 2*sum_p mu_p T_p(e~)]/(pi*h*sqrt(1-e~^2)). The resulting DOS drives equal-mass spectral-window placement (trapezoid CDF quantiles) for the FEAST and pseudopole BSE solvers; solvers/dos.py additionally serves the pseudobands initiative (DOS-weighted window partition per Altman SI Eq. S36 metric n_eff*sigma^{2k}/eps_bar^{2k+1}). NOTE: the entire BSE KPM path is broken at HEAD by a phantom kwarg (see claims); also the stated audit base e18d0e5 differs from the checkout HEAD adc2197 but git diff over all seven touched files between the two commits is empty.

purpose: Windowed pseudopole compression of the correlation screened interaction W_c(omega) in the ISDF r_mu basis, built from the BSE/Casida Hamiltonian: density-biased random seeds (eta -> V eta -> f = d^dag(V eta), non-TDA rhs [f, -fbar]), FEAST contour filtering sum_j w_j (z_j - H)^{-1} x via sharded GMRES, Euclidean whitening, Rayleigh-Ritz in a brightness subspace (eigendecomposition of C_w^H C_w where C_w columns are V(dX+d*Y) density responses), J-metric normalization N_p = ||X_p||^2 - ||Y_p||^2 for the non-Hermitian Casida structure S = [[A,B],[-B*,-A*]], anti-resonant pole doubling (omega -> [Omega, -Omega], d -> [d, conj(d)], w -> [+1, -1] reproducing chi(z) = sum_s [F F^H/(z-Omega) - F* F^T/(z+Omega)]), plus a stochastic tail. Output H5 poles feed W_c[mu,nu](z) = sum_p w_p d_p[mu] conj(d_p[nu])/(z - omega_p) (pseudopoles_eval) and a p_keep convergence sweep vs exact-W references (pseudopoles_sweep). The producer is un-importable at HEAD: three pieces of wiring (two bse_ring_comm functions, the v_couples_k matvec kwarg, the use_nohead loader kwarg) were lost in the src/isdf/bse_isdf -> src/bse consolidation and never restored when the files were resurrected as 'active dev'.
category_guess: pipeline stage (experimental W_c(omega) compression for downstream Sigma/chi, the low-scaling real-axis endgame) — currently dead-in-place due to lost wiring, with two working host-side diagnostics (eval/sweep); NOT dead code per the restore commit fe5e3e8 ("active dev") and the parsed-but-unread-is-lost-wiring rule

purpose: Exact-diagonalization arm of the LORRAX BSE stage: converge the lowest n_eig eigenpairs of the TDA BSE Hamiltonian H = D + V - W in the ISDF pair basis (D[b,c,v,k]=(eps_c[k,c]-eps_v[k,v])X, V = (1/Nk) M* V_q0 M exchange with spin-traced pair amplitude M[k,c,v,mu]=sum_s psi_c*[k,c,s,mu] psi_v[k,v,s,mu], W = FFT-diagonal screened direct term), the LORRAX analogue of BGW absorption.x diagonalization mode. solvers/davidson.py is a shape-agnostic Hermitian block Davidson (QE cegterg-style: ellipsis-einsum Gram projection, Cholesky generalized eigh, CGS2 re-orth, restart at m_max) shared with the DFT NSCF driver; bse_davidson_helpers supplies the lowest-(E_c-E_v)-transition initial subspace and the 1/(dE - lambda + eps) diagonal preconditioner; davidson_absorption is the CLI that produces BGW-format eigenvalues_b{1,2,3}.dat with per-state dipole projections; bse_preconditioner is an older mostly-dead diagonal-preconditioner module whose only live export is energy_diff_cv_k.
category_guess: pipeline stage (BSE eigensolver) layered on shared solver infra — solvers/davidson.py also drives psp/run_nscf DFT NSCF; bse_preconditioner.py is ~90% dead (superseded by bse_feast's sharded reimplementation)

purpose: BSE absorption-spectrum output stage (958 LOC over 4 files): converts a solved TDA BSE Hamiltonian into epsilon_2(omega)/epsilon_1(omega)/JDOS and BGW-format .dat files via two routes plus a comparison tool. Eigenvector route: eps2^a(w) = 16pi^2/(V_cell*N_k*n_spin*n_spinor) * Sum_S |Sum_{kcv} A[S,k,c,v]*d[a,k,c,v]|^2 * (eta/pi)/((w-E_S)^2+eta^2), with d[a,k,c,v] = <c|v_a|v>/(E_c-E_v) sliced from dipole.h5 (host numpy, no jax). Haydock route: 3-polarisation block Lanczos on the sharded TDA matvec HX = D + V - W (lax.scan, alpha_n = Re<s_n|H s_n>, beta_n = ||r_n||), then backward continued fraction g(z)=1/(z-a_0-b_0^2/(z-a_1-...)), eps2 = -pref*||d||^2*Im g(w+i*eta)/pi. eigvals_to_eps2 re-broadens any BGW-format eigenvalues.dat at arbitrary eta/truncation for fair BGW-vs-LORRAX comparison. All math in Ry, file I/O in eV; prefactor verified identical to BGW BSE/absh.f90:46.
category_guess: pipeline stage (post-BSE optics/spectra output); eigvals_to_eps2 doubles as BGW-compat diagnostics

purpose: Five auxiliary-solver files in the physics-free solvers/ package, spanning two unrelated programs. (1) Sternheimer linear-response primitives — projected/preconditioned MINRES (Paige–Saunders), subspace projectors P_U = U·U† and Q_{k−q} = 1 − P_val, the TPA preconditioner P⁻¹_{v,G} = TPA(T_G/K̄²_v) with TPA(x)=(27+18x+12x²+8x³)/(27+18x+12x²+8x³+16x⁴), and the production level-shifted-CG primitive solving A_v·δu = −b with A_v(x) = H_{k−q}x − ε_{v,k}x + α_pv·P_val^{k−q}(x) (QE cgsolve_all convention, custom-JVP-wrapped for ∂χ/∂q) — all serving psp/run_sternheimer.py's χ_{G'0}(q,ω=0) head/wing column, NOT the BSE or GW pipelines. (2) FEAST ellipse contour quadrature for the spectral projector P = (1/2πi)∮(zI−H)⁻¹dz: upper-half midpoint nodes z_j = c + r_x cosθ_j + i r_y sinθ_j, weights w_j = (1/2N)(r_y cosθ_j + i r_x sinθ_j), θ_j = π(2j−1)/(2N) — the only file on the BSE path, consumed by bse/bse_feast.py (which conjugate-augments nodes+weights for full/non-TDA BSE).
category_guess: infra (iterative-solver library): quadrature.py = BSE FEAST eigensolver support; sternheimer_solve/precond + projectors = psp orbital-mag/W0 linear-response primitives (not BSE, not gw/); minres.py and most of projectors.py = superseded/test-only dead weight

purpose: Spectral compression of a Hermitian operator's high-energy manifold into weighted "pseudobands" (LORRAX-native analogue of BGW parabands): a KPM DOS partitions [E_start, E_max] into windows; per window, either random-phase combinations of exact eigenstates (xi_a = (1/sqrt(n)) sum_i exp(i theta_ai) psi_i, exact <xi|H|xi> = mean(E)) or Chebyshev-Jackson filtered blocks (step coefficients gamma_0 = 1 - arccos(b)/pi, gamma_n = -(2/(pi n)) sin(n arccos b), telescoped recurrence T_n = 2 H~ T_{n-1} - T_{n-2}) are Galerkin-Ritz-diagonalized, and each of the k pseudobands gets weight w = sqrt(n_eff/k) absorbed into the stored coefficients (BGW-parabands norm convention). pseudobands.py (v1) is the hybrid stochastic+CJ-Ritz version; pseudobands_v2.py adds shifted-boundary quadratic partition-of-unity windows (n_eff = int w_j(E)^2 rho(E) dE * dim) and Gauss-quadrature (moment->Jacobi->Golub-Welsch) energy nodes for Davidson windows. Both are physics-agnostic over a flat dim = nspinor*ngkmax vector space; the only production caller is psp.run_nscf (writes WFN_pseudobands.h5), with no BSE consumer yet. Files are byte-identical between stated audit base e18d0e5 and working HEAD adc2197.
category_guess: pipeline stage (mean-field band-compression front-end between Davidson NSCF and WFN.h5; v1/v2 is also a variant axis selected by pb_version)

purpose: src/bse/test_bse.py and src/bse/test_davidson_bse.py are argparse CLI smoke scripts (not pytest tests) that exercise the TDA BSE stack H_TDA·X = D + V − W, where D[b,c,v,k]=(ε_c[k,c]−ε_v[k,v])X[b,c,v,k], the q=0 exchange V is built from spin-traced ISDF pair amplitudes M[k,c,v,μ]=Σ_s ψ_c*[k,c,s,μ]ψ_v[k,v,s,μ] contracted through V_q0[μ,ν] (k-diagonally — see claims), and the direct W term is a k-grid FFT convolution of the spinor-resolved encode T[b,μ,ν,t,s,k] with W_R. test_bse benchmarks the single-device matvec and Lanczos against a legacy restart schema (now broken); test_davidson_bse runs the sharded plain-jit matvec + block Davidson on a 2D (x,y) mesh and compares eigenvalues (meV) and gauge-invariant |A^S|² densities (cosine similarity) against a BGW eigenvectors.h5, with zero asserts in either file. The repo-wide gate audit shows neither file is collected by pytest (testpaths=["tests"]) and the entire pytest-collected BSE surface is one read_bgw_eqp round-trip test; every other bse module is ungated.
category_guess: diagnostics (manual CLI smoke/comparison scripts + gate-coverage audit; not pipeline stages)

purpose: Prior-knowledge ledger for the BSE module: STATUS.md records the validated Si 4x4x4 8v-x-8c LORRAX-vs-BGW absorption match (eigenvalues ~3 meV at the ISDF floor, Sigma|d|^2 = 2314.177 machine-match, Haydock peak within 1.5%) plus the BGW-compat index conventions (valence-axis flip, Ry-vs-eV); BGW_COMPARE.md is the cookbook of six conventions any fair comparison must satisfy. The context/ docs carry the physics the code implements — spinor TDA H_BSE = D + V − W with ISDF pair density rho_cv ~ Sigma_mu zeta_mu M_cv(mu,k), the W term as a k−k' FFT convolution per Henneke Eq 4-6 with net 1/Nk normalization — plus three generations of parallel-matvec design (the distributed-X ring scheme was adopted; the 6-axis mesh and block-sharded proposals were not) and FEAST/pseudopole theory and accuracy findings (error dominated by filter response at window boundaries, not GMRES). All files are >=2 months old; five load-bearing claims have rotted vs HEAD (matvec default, V-as-W placeholder, TDA-only, sharding table, FEAST CLI/gamma) and are flagged for verifier re-check.
category_guess: diagnostics — prior-knowledge/comparison-convention documentation (validated-results ledger, BGW-compat cookbook, design history); no runtime role

purpose: End-to-end BSE dataflow in LORRAX: the exciton Hamiltonian H^BSE = D + K^x − K^d is assembled on the coarse GW k-grid entirely from the GW restart bundle tmp/isdf_tensors_{n_rmu}.h5 — D from enk_full (Ry, optionally overwritten by BGW eqp1.dat via SymMaps IBZ→full-BZ unfold), the direct kernel from W0_qmunu (static screened W in the ISDF μν centroid basis, applied as a q=k−k' convolution via an ortho 3-D FFT pair with per-element form (1/Nk)Σ_{k'c'v'}[Σ_tμ ψ*_c(k)ψ_c(k')]W_{μν}(k−k')[Σ_sν ψ_v(k)ψ*_v(k')]X(k')), the exchange from the V_qmunu[q=0] tile with the q→0 head reinstated as a rank-1 (head/V_cell)·conj(G0)⊗G0 update. Trial vectors X(b,c,v,k) are sharded P(None,'x','y',None) on a 2-D device mesh with ψ held in dual μ-on-x / ν-on-y copies; solvers are TDA Lanczos/block-Lanczos/Davidson (plus FEAST/KPM/pseudopole diagnostics), and spectra come from Haydock continued fractions or eigenvector sum-over-states against dipole.h5, written in BGW-compatible formats (eV, valence-axis flip at file boundaries only).
category_guess: pipeline stage (post-GW BSE solve + spectra), with a diagnostics constellation (FEAST/KPM/pseudopoles/w_exact sweeps) around a single kernel

purpose: Complete user-facing input surface of the LORRAX BSE stack (src/bse + src/solvers): the CLI flags, cohsex.in keys, and env vars that steer the sharded iterative solution of H·X = D·X + V·X − W·X (bse_jax.py:86), where ΔE[c,v,k]=eps_c[k,c]−eps_v[k,v] scales X, the exchange term contracts pair densities M[k,c,v,μ]=Σ_s conj(ψ_c[k,c,s,μ])ψ_v[k,v,s,μ] against the q=0 Coulomb block V_q0[μ,ν], and the direct term applies W(q=k−k′) via 3-D FFT convolution over the k-grid. Unlike gw/, BSE has no LorraxConfig section: every CLI is argparse-driven, and the only cohsex.in keys read are wfn_file, vhead, whead_0freq via two private ad-hoc parsers in bse_io.py (gw_config/read_lorrax_input is never imported). All energies are Ry internally with explicit --ry-to-ev/--units-ev-per-ry conversion flags; valence-axis flip and Ry→eV on eigenvector write are deliberate BGW-compat conventions (STATUS.md "Index ordering"), not bugs.
category_guess: config — cross-cutting input/flag surface for the BSE pipeline stage (plus diagnostics-only sweep CLIs)

purpose: Reference deep-read of BerkeleyGW's coarse-to-fine BSE machinery (kernel.x + absorption.x) that LORRAX's single-grid BSE lacks. Physics: fine states are expanded in coarse ones via overlap coefficients d_{n,k_fi}^{m,k_co} = Σ_{G,spinor} c_fi(G,n)·conj(c_co(G+G_umk,m)) with G_umk = nint(k_fi−k_co) (R&L Eq. 39); the coarse kernel K^d = Σ_GG' M*_cc'(G) W_GG'(q) M_v'v(G') is stored as head/wing/body with the divergent factors stripped (head stored as 1, wing multiplied by |q_co|), then interpolated as K_fi = Σ dvv_k·conj(dcc_k)·K_co·conj(dvv_k')·dcc_k' and re-multiplied by analytic w_eff = ⟨v(q_fi)⟩_mBZ·ε⁻¹₀₀(q_fi) (head, wcoul0/(8π) at q=0) and 1/q_fi (wing) at the fine q = k−k'. Also documents ε⁻¹-diagonal tabulation over q+G points (epsdiag), the mini-BZ Monte-Carlo v(q) average with analytic small-sphere correction, finite-Q (valence-shifted, exchange at v(G−Q), COM momentum = −Q), the CSI clustered-subsampling replacement below a |q| cutoff, and the non-TDA coupling-block interpolation (conjugation swap + primed-index swap, requires extended_kernel). Deliverable: reports/bse_refactor_map_2026-07-15/archive/files/bgw_fine_grid_reference.md.
category_guess: pipeline stage (external reference crib sheet — BGW fine-grid BSE interpolation pipeline that LORRAX bse/ does not yet implement)
